topRanked.journalTitle: Mammal Species of the World: a Taxonomic and Geographic Reference (3 rd Edition), Volume 1
topRanked.speciesDesc: Laephotis botswanae is a species of long-eared bat found in sub-Saharan Africa, with its type locality being Botswana. It is also known as the Botswanan Long-eared Bat and has been recorded in several countries including Zambia, Malawi, Zimbabwe, and South Africa. The conservation status of Laephotis botswanae is currently Lower Risk on the IUCN Red List.

topRanked.articleTitle: The bats of the Congo and of Rwanda and Burundi revisited (Mammalia: Chiroptera)
topRanked.articleAuthor: Cakenberghe, Victor Van, Tungaluna, Guy-Crispin Gembu, Akawa, Prescott Musaba, Seamark, Ernest & Verheyen, Erik
topRanked.speciesDesc: Laephotis botswanae is a species of bird in the family Balaenicipitidae, found in southern Africa. It was first described by Setzer in 1971 and has since been reported from several localities in Zambia, Zimbabwe, Botswana, Namibia, Malawi, Angola, and South Africa. The exact distribution and relationship between Laephotis botswanae and Laephotis angolensis are not well understood due to the scarcity of data on the former species.

topRanked.articleTitle: The bats of the Congo and of Rwanda and Burundi revisited (Mammalia: Chiroptera)
topRanked.articleAuthor: Cakenberghe, Victor Van, Tungaluna, Guy-Crispin Gembu, Akawa, Prescott Musaba, Seamark, Ernest & Verheyen, Erik
topRanked.speciesDesc: Laephotis angolensis is a subspecies of leopard with distinct cranial characteristics, making it different from Laephotis wintoni angolensis. It was previously considered monotypic but has since been found to be specifically different based on cranial features. The species is known from Angola, Zambia, Botswana, and the southern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

response.answer: The provided documents do not contain information about where Laephotis botswanae roosts.
